hazard.summary: Provider returns 429 with Retry-After but client ignores header and continues calling, wasting retries and thrashing fallback.
signals: fallback to smart tier; next batch retries Groq immediately; repeat

constraints: If response includes Retry-After, the client MUST honor it (sleep or short-circuit) before making another call to that provider.
Rate limit cooldown MUST be scoped to the correct key (provider + model + org/project/api key as applicable), not global across unrelated traffic.
Retry attempts MUST be budgeted so repeated 429s cannot burn the full retry envelope without waiting.
verification: Unit test: simulate httpx.HTTPStatusError(429) with Retry-After=183; assert next send_async() makes no HTTP call until time passes.
Integration test: run N batches; confirm Groq is skipped during cooldown and fallback is not thrashed each batch.
Telemetry: emit and log groq_rate_limited_until and cooldown_key; verify Scan can attribute clustered 429s to a single cooldown event.
notes: Fix sketch: read Retry-After in groq.py 429 handler, propagate as structured metadata, store _rate_limited_until on GroqClient, and short-circuit send_async() during cooldown.
